fsm_model.description: FSM model for Ecosystem Information Cluster managing device names and location metadata for bridged devices with conflict resolution and user consent enforcement

states.name: DeviceUninitialized
states.description: No EcosystemDeviceStruct exists for this device yet

states.name: DeviceRegisteredInaccessible
states.description: Device struct created but device not accessible through bridge (no BridgedEndpoint)

states.name: DeviceRegisteredAccessible
states.description: Device struct created and device is accessible through bridge (BridgedEndpoint present and valid)

states.name: DeviceNameAbsent
states.description: No device name has been set (default state, DeviceName field empty)

states.name: DeviceNamePresent
states.description: Device name has been set with user consent, timestamp tracks last modification

states.name: LocationUnassigned
states.description: Device not assigned to any locations (UniqueLocationIDs list is empty)

states.name: LocationAssigned
states.description: Device assigned to one or more locations (1-64 location IDs)
invariants: forall id in unique_location_ids: location_exists_in_directory(id) == true

states.name: LocationStable
states.description: No pending location changes, system in steady state

states.name: LocationRelocationPending
states.description: External user interaction moved device to different physical location, awaiting UniqueLocationID change

states.name: LocationRenamePending
states.description: External user interaction renamed location but same physical location, UniqueLocationID must remain unchanged

states.name: RemoteSyncPending
states.description: Receiving location data from remote server instance, determining if UniqueLocationID should change

states.name: ConsentNotGiven
states.description: User has not consented to external ecosystem providing device names and locations

states.name: ConsentGiven
states.description: User has consented to external ecosystem providing device names and locations

states.name: NoConflict
states.description: Normal operation with no concurrent update conflicts detected

states.name: ConcurrentUpdateDetected
states.description: Multiple server instances updated UniqueLocationIDs simultaneously, conflict resolution required

states.name: LocationDirectoryEmpty
states.description: LocationDirectory attribute contains no EcosystemLocationStruct entries

states.name: LocationDirectoryPopulated
states.description: LocationDirectory contains one or more EcosystemLocationStruct entries defining available locations

states.name: LocationDefinitionStable
states.description: A specific location definition is stable with no pending changes

states.name: LocationDescriptorUpdatePending
states.description: LocationDescriptor for a location is being updated with new value

transitions.guard_condition: is_matter_device == true && valid_endpoint(original_ep) == true && valid_endpoint(bridged_ep) == true
user_consent_given == true && location_exists_in_directory(location_id) == true && length(unique_location_ids) < 64 && contains(unique_location_ids, location_id) == false

functions.name: create_ecosystem_device_struct
functions.description: Allocates and initializes a new EcosystemDeviceStruct with all mandatory fields set to defaults
functions.algorithm_detail: Allocates memory for struct, sets DeviceName to empty string, DeviceNameLastEdit to 0, DeviceTypes to empty list, UniqueLocationIDs to empty list, UniqueLocationIDsLastEdit to 0, BridgedEndpoint and OriginalEndpoint to null
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T1, T2, T3 when registering new device

functions.name: set_device_types

parameters.name: device_type_list
parameters.description: List of device types from Descriptor cluster's DeviceTypeList

functions.description: Sets the DeviceTypes field to match the provided list from Descriptor cluster
functions.algorithm_detail: Validates each device type ID in list is a valid Matter device type identifier, then performs deep copy of list to DeviceTypes field
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T1, T2, T3 during device registration and T23, T23b when Descriptor changes

functions.name: initialize_timestamps_to_zero
functions.description: Sets all timestamp fields to 0 indicating no user modifications yet
functions.algorithm_detail: DeviceNameLastEdit := 0, UniqueLocationIDsLastEdit := 0. Zero timestamp indicates field has not been modified by user
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T1, T2, T3 during initial device registration

functions.name: set_bridged_endpoint

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: Valid endpoint number on this bridge

functions.description: Sets the BridgedEndpoint field to the specified valid endpoint number
functions.algorithm_detail: Validates endpoint exists in bridge's endpoint table and is reachable, then assigns BridgedEndpoint field to endpoint value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T2, T3, T4 when device becomes accessible through bridge

functions.name: set_original_endpoint

parameters.name: original_ep
parameters.description: Valid endpoint number on the original Matter device

functions.description: Sets the OriginalEndpoint field for Matter devices to preserve endpoint identity across bridge hops
functions.algorithm_detail: Validates endpoint is valid on original device, then assigns OriginalEndpoint field to original_ep value. This value must remain unchanged if device is bridged through multiple hops
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T3 when registering Matter device with known original endpoint

functions.name: clear_bridged_endpoint
functions.description: Removes BridgedEndpoint field when device is no longer accessible through bridge
functions.algorithm_detail: Sets BridgedEndpoint field to null or removes field from struct
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T5 when device becomes inaccessible

functions.name: set_device_name

parameters.name: name
parameters.description: Device name provided by user, maximum 64 characters

functions.description: Sets the DeviceName field to the provided name after validation
functions.algorithm_detail: Validates length(name) <= 64 characters, validates UTF-8 encoding, then assigns DeviceName field to name value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T6, T8 when user sets or updates device name

functions.name: set_device_name_last_edit

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Microseconds since Unix epoch when DeviceName was last modified

functions.description: Updates the timestamp of when DeviceName was last modified for conflict resolution
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns DeviceNameLastEdit field to timestamp value. Must be monotonically increasing for conflict resolution to work correctly
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T6, T8, T9 whenever DeviceName changes

functions.name: current_timestamp
functions.description: Returns the current system time in microseconds since Unix epoch
functions.algorithm_detail: Queries system monotonic clock, converts to microseconds since Unix epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC), returns as 64-bit unsigned integer
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called throughout FSM to generate timestamps for conflict resolution (T6, T8, T10, T11, T12, T12b, T14, T16, T18, T20, T22)

functions.name: generate_event

parameters.name: event_name
parameters.description: Name of event to generate for observers/clients

functions.description: Generates a named event for observers/clients to be notified of state changes
functions.algorithm_detail: Creates event object with event_name and current timestamp, publishes to Matter event notification system for subscribed clients
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called throughout FSM to notify clients of changes (device_name_updated, location_assigned, consent_required_error, etc.)

functions.name: clear_device_name
functions.description: Removes the DeviceName field, reverting to default empty state
functions.algorithm_detail: Sets DeviceName field to empty string (default value)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T9 when device name is explicitly cleared

functions.name: location_exists_in_directory

parameters.name: location_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID to search for in LocationDirectory attribute

functions.description: Checks if the given UniqueLocationID exists in LocationDirectory attribute to prevent dangling references
functions.algorithm_detail: Searches LocationDirectory attribute for EcosystemLocationStruct entry with UniqueLocationID matching location_id, returns true if found, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T10, T11 to validate location references before assignment

functions.name: add_to_unique_location_ids

parameters.name: location_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID to add to the list, maximum 64 characters

functions.description: Adds a location ID to the UniqueLocationIDs list after validation
functions.algorithm_detail: Validates length(location_id) <= 64 and length(UniqueLocationIDs) < 64, then appends location_id to UniqueLocationIDs list
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T10, T11 when assigning device to locations

functions.name: set_unique_location_ids_last_edit

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Microseconds since Unix epoch when UniqueLocationIDs was last modified

functions.description: Updates the timestamp of when UniqueLocationIDs was last modified for conflict resolution
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns UniqueLocationIDsLastEdit field to timestamp value. Used for last-write-wins conflict resolution between multiple server instances
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T10, T11, T12, T12b, T22 whenever UniqueLocationIDs changes

functions.name: contains

parameters.name: list
parameters.description: List to search

parameters.name: item
parameters.description: Item to search for

functions.description: Checks if item is present in list
functions.algorithm_detail: Iterates through list elements, compares each with item using string equality, returns true if match found, false if iteration completes without match
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T11, T12, T12b to check for duplicate locations or validate removal

functions.name: remove_from_unique_location_ids

parameters.name: location_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID to remove from the list

functions.description: Removes a location ID from the UniqueLocationIDs list
functions.algorithm_detail: Searches UniqueLocationIDs list for location_id, removes entry if found, compacts list to eliminate gap
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T12, T12b when unassigning device from location

functions.name: is_external_interaction
functions.description: Determines if the current change originated from external user interaction vs internal/remote system change
functions.algorithm_detail: Checks change source context: returns true if change originated from user action in external ecosystem interface, false if originated from internal Matter system operation or remote server synchronization
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T13, T15 to distinguish external user actions from system operations for UniqueLocationID change policy

functions.name: represents_different_location

parameters.name: old_location
parameters.description: Previous location descriptor

parameters.name: new_location
parameters.description: New location descriptor

functions.description: Determines if two location descriptors represent different physical/logical locations vs just rename of same location
functions.algorithm_detail: Compares semantic meaning of locations using heuristics: different room names, area types, or location metadata indicate different locations. Case differences, whitespace, or minor formatting changes indicate same location. Returns true if semantically different, false if same location with different name
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T13 to determine if location change requires new UniqueLocationID (relocation) or not (rename)

functions.name: store_new_location_descriptor

parameters.name: new_location
parameters.description: New location descriptor to store temporarily

functions.description: Temporarily stores new location descriptor pending processing
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns pending_location temporary variable to new_location value for later processing
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T13 to store location during relocation pending state

functions.name: generate_new_unique_location_id
functions.description: Generates a new unique identifier for the location, used during relocation to different physical location
functions.algorithm_detail: Uses cryptographically secure random number generator (DRBG) to generate random bytes, encodes as alphanumeric string up to 64 characters. Ensures uniqueness within this server instance by checking against existing UniqueLocationIDs. Assigns generated ID to UniqueLocationID field
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T14 when processing relocation to ensure new location gets new ID

functions.name: set_location_descriptor

parameters.name: location
parameters.description: Location descriptor value to set

functions.description: Sets the LocationDescriptor field to the provided value
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns LocationDescriptor field to location value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T14, T16, T18, T20 when updating location information

functions.name: set_location_descriptor_last_edit

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Microseconds since Unix epoch when LocationDescriptor was last modified

functions.description: Updates the timestamp of when LocationDescriptor was last modified
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns LocationDescriptorLastEdit field to timestamp value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T14, T16, T18, T20 whenever LocationDescriptor changes

functions.name: same_physical_location

parameters.name: old_name
parameters.description: Previous location name

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.description: New location name

functions.description: Determines if two location names refer to the same physical location (rename) vs different locations (relocation)
functions.algorithm_detail: Returns true if names are semantically equivalent (e.g., case differences, whitespace variations, formatting changes only). Returns false if names represent different physical locations (e.g., bedroom vs kitchen). Uses semantic comparison, not just string equality
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T15 to determine if location change is a rename (keep UniqueLocationID) or relocation (change UniqueLocationID)

functions.name: store_new_location_name

parameters.name: new_name
parameters.description: New location name to store temporarily

functions.description: Temporarily stores new location name pending processing during rename
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns pending_location_name temporary variable to new_name value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T15 to store name during rename pending state

functions.name: keep_unique_location_id_unchanged
functions.description: Explicitly maintains the current UniqueLocationID unchanged (no-op for documentation and clarity)
functions.algorithm_detail: No change to UniqueLocationID field. This function is explicitly called to document that UniqueLocationID is intentionally not changed during rename or remote sync scenarios
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T16, T20 to explicitly document that UniqueLocationID must not change

functions.name: store_remote_location_data

parameters.name: remote_location
parameters.description: Location descriptor from remote server instance

parameters.name: remote_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID from remote server instance

functions.description: Stores location data received from remote server instance for synchronization processing
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns pending_remote_location to remote_location and pending_remote_id to remote_id for later processing
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T17, T19 when receiving remote sync data

functions.name: set_unique_location_id

parameters.name: location_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID value to set, maximum 64 characters

functions.description: Sets the UniqueLocationID field
functions.algorithm_detail: Validates length(location_id) <= 64, then assigns UniqueLocationID field to location_id value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T18 when applying remote sync that changed UniqueLocationID

functions.name: multiple_instances_updating_simultaneously
functions.description: Detects if multiple server instances are updating UniqueLocationIDs at the same time causing potential conflict
functions.algorithm_detail: Checks update timestamps and sources: if multiple updates from different server instances have timestamps within small time window (e.g., less than clock synchronization tolerance), returns true indicating concurrent update conflict. Otherwise returns false
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T21 to detect concurrent update conflicts

functions.name: mark_conflict_state
functions.description: Marks system as being in conflict resolution state
functions.algorithm_detail: Sets conflict_detected flag to true
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T21 when entering conflict state

functions.name: store_competing_updates

parameters.name: update_list
parameters.description: List of all competing concurrent updates to be resolved

functions.description: Stores all competing updates for conflict resolution using last-write-wins strategy
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns competing_updates list to update_list. Each UpdateRecord contains: source_instance_id, timestamp, new_unique_location_ids value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T21 to store competing updates

functions.name: apply_update_with_highest_timestamp
functions.description: Selects and applies the update with the highest timestamp using last-write-wins conflict resolution
functions.algorithm_detail: Sorts competing_updates list by timestamp in descending order, selects first entry (highest timestamp), applies that update's new_unique_location_ids value to UniqueLocationIDs field
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T22 during conflict resolution

functions.name: drop_other_updates
functions.description: Discards all updates except the winning update in conflict resolution
functions.algorithm_detail: Removes all entries from competing_updates list except the applied update. Per spec NOTE, this may result in one or more updates being permanently lost, which is acceptable
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T22 to discard losing updates

functions.name: clear_external_data
functions.description: Removes all externally-provided data when user consent is revoked
functions.algorithm_detail: Sets DeviceName to empty, LocationDescriptor to empty or default, clears all fields that required user consent to be populated
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T25 when user revokes consent for external ecosystem data

functions.name: valid_endpoint

parameters.name: endpoint
parameters.description: Endpoint number to validate

functions.description: Validates that an endpoint number is valid and reachable on this bridge or device
functions.algorithm_detail: Checks if endpoint exists in bridge's endpoint table, verifies endpoint is currently reachable and not in error state, returns true if valid and accessible, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T2, T3, T4 to validate endpoints before setting BridgedEndpoint or OriginalEndpoint

functions.name: length

parameters.name: item
parameters.description: String or list to measure

functions.description: Returns the length of a string (character count) or list (element count)
functions.algorithm_detail: For string: counts UTF-8 characters (not bytes). For list: counts number of elements
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called throughout FSM to validate length constraints (T6, T8, T10, T11, T12, T12b)

functions.name: set_external_change_pending

parameters.name: pending
parameters.description: Whether an external location change is pending

functions.description: Marks whether an external location change is pending processing
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns external_change_pending flag to pending value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T13, T14, T15, T16 to track external change state

functions.name: set_remote_sync_in_progress

parameters.name: in_progress
parameters.description: Whether a remote sync operation is in progress

functions.description: Marks whether a remote sync operation is in progress
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns remote_sync_in_progress flag to in_progress value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T17, T18, T19, T20 to track remote sync state

functions.name: set_user_consent_given

parameters.name: consent
parameters.description: Whether user consent is given

functions.description: Sets the user consent flag indicating permission to receive external ecosystem data
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns user_consent_given flag to consent value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T24, T25 when user grants or revokes consent

functions.name: user_interaction_confirmed
functions.description: Verifies that a user action was authentic and not spoofed by attacker
functions.algorithm_detail: Checks authentication of user action source using Matter's authentication mechanisms. Verifies action originated from authenticated user session with appropriate privileges. Returns true if verified as legitimate user input, false if authentication fails or source is untrusted
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T24, T25 to validate user consent actions are authentic

functions.name: create_ecosystem_location_struct

parameters.name: unique_location_id
parameters.description: Unique identifier for this location, maximum 64 characters

parameters.name: location_descriptor
parameters.description: Location descriptor with name and metadata

functions.description: Creates a new EcosystemLocationStruct entry for LocationDirectory
functions.algorithm_detail: Allocates memory for struct, sets UniqueLocationID to unique_location_id, LocationDescriptor to location_descriptor, LocationDescriptorLastEdit to 0 (will be set by caller)
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T26, T27 when adding location to directory

functions.name: set_location_descriptor_last_edit_in_struct

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Microseconds since Unix epoch

functions.description: Sets LocationDescriptorLastEdit field in a newly created EcosystemLocationStruct
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns LocationDescriptorLastEdit field to timestamp value in the struct being created
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T26, T27 when creating new location entries

functions.name: add_to_location_directory

parameters.name: location_struct
parameters.description: Location struct to add to directory

functions.description: Adds an EcosystemLocationStruct to the LocationDirectory attribute
functions.algorithm_detail: Validates struct fields are valid, checks UniqueLocationID doesn't already exist in directory, then appends location_struct to LocationDirectory list
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T26, T27 when adding new locations

functions.name: remove_from_location_directory

parameters.name: unique_location_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID of location to remove

functions.description: Removes an EcosystemLocationStruct from LocationDirectory attribute
functions.algorithm_detail: Searches LocationDirectory for entry with matching UniqueLocationID, removes entry if found, compacts list
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T28, T28b when removing locations from directory

functions.name: location_in_use_by_devices

parameters.name: unique_location_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID to check

functions.description: Checks if any device is currently assigned to this location
functions.algorithm_detail: Searches all EcosystemDeviceStruct entries, checks if unique_location_id appears in any UniqueLocationIDs list. Returns true if location is referenced by at least one device, false otherwise
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T28, T28b to prevent removing locations that are still in use

functions.name: set_location_change_pending

parameters.name: pending
parameters.description: Whether a location descriptor change is pending

functions.description: Marks whether a location descriptor update is pending
functions.algorithm_detail: Assigns location_change_pending flag to pending value
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transitions T29, T30 to track location descriptor update state

functions.name: update_location_descriptor_in_directory

parameters.name: unique_location_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID of location to update

parameters.name: new_descriptor
parameters.description: New LocationDescriptor value

functions.description: Updates the LocationDescriptor field of a location in LocationDirectory
functions.algorithm_detail: Searches LocationDirectory for entry with matching UniqueLocationID, updates LocationDescriptor field to new_descriptor
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T30 when applying location descriptor updates

functions.name: set_location_descriptor_last_edit_in_directory

parameters.name: unique_location_id
parameters.description: UniqueLocationID of location to update

parameters.name: timestamp
parameters.description: Timestamp of last edit

functions.description: Updates the LocationDescriptorLastEdit timestamp for a location in LocationDirectory
functions.algorithm_detail: Searches LocationDirectory for entry with matching UniqueLocationID, updates LocationDescriptorLastEdit field to timestamp
functions.usage_in_fsm: Called in transition T30 when applying location descriptor updates

definitions.definition: Data access is restricted to principals within the same Matter fabric. Different fabrics cannot access each other's data even on the same physical device
definitions.security_relevance: Provides isolation between different administrative domains sharing the same bridge infrastructure

definitions.definition: Attribute containing all EcosystemLocationStruct entries available on this server instance. Acts as the authoritative source of location definitions
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents dangling location references by requiring all UniqueLocationIDs to reference existing directory entries

definitions.definition: Timestamp type representing microseconds since Unix epoch (1970-01-01 00:00:00 UTC) as 64-bit unsigned integer
definitions.security_relevance: Used for conflict resolution via last-write-wins. Clock synchronization critical for security

definitions.definition: Device type indicating endpoint represents a bridged device from another ecosystem or non-Matter device
definitions.security_relevance: Required device type for endpoints hosting Ecosystem Information Cluster

definitions.definition: Matter cluster providing device type information via DeviceTypeList attribute
definitions.security_relevance: Source of truth for device capabilities. Must remain consistent with DeviceTypes field

definitions.definition: User action originating from external ecosystem interface (e.g., companion app, cloud service) rather than Matter system internal operation
definitions.security_relevance: Determines whether location change is relocation (UniqueLocationID changes) or rename (UniqueLocationID unchanged)

definitions.definition: Last-write-wins strategy using timestamps to resolve concurrent updates from multiple server instances
definitions.security_relevance: Prevents split-brain scenarios but may lose updates if clock skew is large

definitions.definition: Individual Ecosystem Information Cluster implementation on a specific bridge. Multiple instances may exist for same device across different bridges
definitions.security_relevance: No guarantee of UniqueLocationID consistency across instances. Only single-instance history should be trusted

security_properties.description: Data in EcosystemDeviceStruct and EcosystemLocationStruct is fabric-scoped, preventing cross-fabric access and information leakage
critical_transitions: All transitions - fabric isolation enforced at access layer

security_properties.description: External data (device names, locations) can only be set or modified with explicit user consent
When multiple server instances provide conflicting data, the entry with highest timestamp wins using last-write-wins strategy
DeviceTypes field must always match Descriptor cluster's DeviceTypeList to ensure consistent device capability representation
All UniqueLocationIDs in device struct must reference existing EcosystemLocationStruct entries in LocationDirectory to prevent dangling references
BridgedEndpoint field present if and only if device is accessible through bridge, ensuring correct command routing
OriginalEndpoint must be preserved across bridge hops for Matter devices to maintain traceability to source device
UniqueLocationID changes when device relocated to different physical location, but remains unchanged when location is renamed
Local UniqueLocationID change policy follows remote instance's change policy during synchronization
Timestamps must increase monotonically with each modification to enable proper conflict resolution

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: Cryptographically secure random string generation using system DRBG
cryptographic_operations.output: String up to 64 characters, alphanumeric, cryptographically random
assumptions: System DRBG is secure and properly seeded

cryptographic_operations.algorithm: System monotonic clock with microsecond resolution
assumptions: System clock not manipulated by attacker
Reasonable clock synchronization across nodes (< 1 second drift)
Clock provides monotonic guarantee within single node

security_assumptions.assumption: Matter's fabric-scoped access control cryptography is secure and correctly implemented at the access layer
security_assumptions.if_violated: Cross-fabric data leakage enables reconnaissance and privacy violations (SP1 fails)

security_assumptions.assumption: System clocks across server instances are reasonably synchronized (clock drift < conflict resolution tolerance)
security_assumptions.if_violated: Conflict resolution fails, stale data wins, or updates applied out of order (SP3, SP10 fail)

security_assumptions.assumption: User consent actions are authenticated using Matter's authentication mechanisms and cannot be spoofed by attacker
security_assumptions.if_violated: Attacker can inject or modify device names and locations without user permission (SP2 fails)

security_assumptions.assumption: Multiple simultaneous updates from different server instances are unlikely in normal operation (per specification NOTE)
security_assumptions.if_violated: More than one update may be lost during conflict resolution, causing data corruption beyond acceptable threshold

security_assumptions.assumption: Descriptor cluster changes propagate reliably to Ecosystem Information cluster via internal event mechanism
security_assumptions.if_violated: DeviceTypes diverge from actual device capabilities, enabling privilege escalation (SP4 fails)

security_assumptions.assumption: LocationDirectory attribute is properly maintained, synchronized, and contains all referenced location entries
security_assumptions.if_violated: Dangling location references cause client errors and device location information loss (SP5 fails)

security_assumptions.assumption: Endpoint validity checks accurately reflect current device accessibility and reachability status
security_assumptions.if_violated: Commands routed to wrong or unreachable devices, causing failures or security issues (SP6 fails)

security_assumptions.assumption: System can reliably distinguish external user interaction from internal system changes or remote synchronization
security_assumptions.if_violated: Relocation vs rename incorrectly determined, UniqueLocationID change policy violated (SP8 fails)

notes.parallel_state_machines: This FSM is actually a composition of 7 parallel state machines: (1) Device Registration, (2) Device Name, (3) Location Assignment, (4) Location Change (device-location association), (5) Consent, (6) Conflict Resolution, (7) LocationDirectory Management, (8) Location Definition (EcosystemLocationStruct). These operate independently but share state variables.
notes.acceptable_data_loss: Per specification NOTE, when multiple server instances update UniqueLocationIDs simultaneously, it is possible one of the updates will be missed. This is considered an acceptable limitation.
notes.no_cross_instance_consistency: UniqueLocationID values may differ across server instances for the same physical location. Only single-instance history should be used for change detection.
notes.timestamp_critical: Entire conflict resolution mechanism depends on accurate, synchronized timestamps. Clock skew is major vulnerability.
notes.fabric_scoped_isolation: All data structures are fabric-scoped. This FSM assumes Matter's fabric isolation is correctly enforced at access layer.
notes.location_directory_vs_device_assignment: The FSM models two distinct concerns: (1) LocationDirectory management - creating/updating/removing location definitions (EcosystemLocationStruct), and (2) Device-location associations - assigning devices to existing locations via UniqueLocationIDs references.
